title: Peer-audit state
description: Per-audit state file written to <target-dir>/.peer-audit-<slug>.json. Tracks pass history, per-rater scores, findings, and convergence status for the peer-audit skill and its N-rater generalization (Triad). The rater set is open: any number of named engines (opus/codex/gemini/...) may participate, recorded under a `raters` map keyed by engine name. The two-rater (codex+claude) shape stays valid: legacy per-pass `codex`/`claude` blocks are still accepted, and a reader may mirror them into the `raters` map.

slug.description: Kebab-case slug derived from subject. Used in filenames.

subject.description: Human-readable subject string. Echoed in prompts.

max_passes.description: Hard ceiling; if exceeded, Phase 5 fires with DID-NOT-CONVERGE.

raters.description: The engines participating in this audit, in display order. For the classic two-rater peer-audit this is ['codex','claude']; for Triad it is e.g. ['opus','codex','gemini']. Engine names are lowercase tokens. The convergence gate requires EVERY name in this list to hit GO + target on the last pass. If absent (legacy 2-rater state), readers fall back to ['codex','claude'].

convergence.description: Convergence target + status. target_craft/target_fit are STORED USER VALUES (the bar the user set when launching the audit), not literals baked into the scripts — the gate reads them from here.

target_craft.description: User-set craft bar every rater must meet to converge.

target_fit.description: User-set fit bar every rater must meet to converge.

last_reasoning.description: Human-readable explanation of the last convergence decision.

codex_binary.description: Path to codex CLI if detected, null if paste-mode.

history.description: Append-only per-pass record. Each entry carries a `raters` map keyed by engine name. For backward compatibility the classic flat `codex`/`claude` blocks are also accepted and, when present, are mirrored into the `raters` map by update_state.py.

raters.description: Map of engine-name -> per-rater result for this pass. Replaces the hardcoded codex/claude keys; an arbitrary number of engines is allowed.

codex.description: LEGACY 2-rater block. Still written alongside `raters['codex']` for backward compatibility so old readers (and the classic peer-audit) keep working.

claude.description: LEGACY 2-rater block. Still written alongside `raters['claude']` for backward compatibility.

open_findings.description: Findings carried into the next pass. Train 8 removed the unused `id` field from `required` to match what parse_polish_output.py actually emits and what update_state.py writes into live state.

introduced_by.description: Engine name that introduced the finding (codex/claude/gemini/...).
